A long time ago, in the vast city of
Johannesburg, a baby girl was born.
That baby was me. My mom called me
Miriam. Miriam Makeba.
< % ee <

Mom was a sangoma, but she also
worked to make other people's homes
neat and clean. It was hard for my
mom to earn enough for both of us. She
started selling homemade beer to bring
IN more money.

The laws of the land said that selling
homemade beer was wrong. The police
sent my mom To jail for six long months.
| was only 18 days old, and needed my
mom. So even though | was just a baby,
| went to jail too.

As a little girl | loved to sing. When
| was older, | heloed my mom clean
houses. Singing songs as | worked made
the chores go faster and the days seem
brighter. Singing made me happier than
| can explain.
“oo £

| sang in my church, and this made
others happy too. Music has the power
TO bring people together. When we were
SINGING we felt brave and sTrONng.

a .

People said my voice was a gift and my
songs were special. | sang with other
musicians and our music was heard all
over the world.

My home was Sophiatown, a place
of culture and music. Sophiatown, oa
olace where South Africans could make
Music in harmony and dance together.
But the people who ruled the land at
the time did not like this togetherness.
Those rulers didn't want black and white
oeople to be friends. \

| knew it was wrong to treat people
differently because of their skin colour.
| did not hide my beliefs, and so those
oeople In charge wanted me out
of the COUNTTY.
When | was singing in America, | was
told | could never return home.

People all over the world heard My story.
My songs and my story heloed many
to see how there was no fairness in
South Africa for those with black skin. |
decided to go on singing and telling the
truth about my country, no matter what.

The world loved my music and | was
welcomed in many countries. | won
awards and sang for important oeople
all over the globe. My life was good, but
something was missing. | could not sing
In my home COUNTTY, and oeople there
were not free.

Then a wonderful day dawned when
Nelson Mandela became president
of South Africa. New people were in
charge and the unfair laws belonged TO
the past. | finally went home with new
hope IN my heart.

After that | could sINg IN a free, fair
country. People of different skin colours
could enjoy music together. | heloed
make this hagopen because | was brave
and strong. | sang the truth in all of
my songs.
